title: link_frame.json + schnorr48.json consumed by Python only; Rust/C hardcode (C omits validation-strength vectors)
description: Python: test_vectors.py:92-117 (link_frame, 4 vectors, verified passing) and :533-551 (schnorr48, 15 vectors). Rust: re-encodes the same 4 frame wires + 14/15 schnorr vectors as hardcoded tests (frame.rs:384-447, schnorr.rs tests) - never loads JSON. C: frame tests (lichen/tests/frame/main.c) contain ZERO wire vectors (NULL/metadata only); schnorr48 tests hardcode 10/15, omitting exactly identity-pubkey, low-order, non-canonical-s, zero-s, truncated (see security bead mmgu.2). Also: two vector stores exist with split consumers - spec/test-vectors/frame.json (parse-focused, consumed only by Rust frame.rs:479-480) vs test/vectors/link_frame.json (encode-focused, Python only); spec/test-vectors/schnorr48.json and test/vectors/schnorr48.json have byte-identical bodies but independent metadata and can drift separately. And test/vectors/generate.py has NO schnorr48 generator (no in-tree oracle regeneration path). Fix: add JSON loaders (or codegen with CI diff) to Rust lichen-link and C frame/schnorr48 suites; consolidate the duplicate stores.
